states.name: isMobile
states.initial: false during SSR because window is absent

states.name: state
states.initial: expanded when defaultOpen true, otherwise collapsed; peeking while transient peek is active

states.name: width
states.initial: defaultWidth, clamped only when setWidth is called

semanticGraph.root: Provider wrapper div; desktop Sidebar root is aside, mobile root is portaled backdrop plus nav
aria: all Sidebar descendants require Provider; useSidebar outside it throws
desktop collapse modes are icon, offcanvas, or none; none always renders expanded aside
viewport below mobileBreakpoint renders a modal-like nav sheet labelled Navigation instead of aside
closed mobile nav has aria-hidden=true and is made inert by ref effect; open focuses first focusable descendant or nav
Escape closes mobile sheet and restores prior focus; focus leaving open sheet closes it
Trigger exposes aria-expanded and Collapse sidebar/Expand sidebar label; Rail is tabIndex -1; ResizeHandle is keyboard focusable
Collapsible trigger receives aria-expanded and aria-controls; content stays mounted as role=region and closed content has aria-hidden=true plus inert after ref effect
inactive SlidingView stays mounted with aria-hidden=true, pointer-events-none and inert after ref effect
MenuButton/MenuSubButton auto-wrap in li when not already inside corresponding item

transitions.effect: toggles desktop open, or mobile openMobile; desktop toggle stops peeking

transitions.action: hover/focus enters collapsed desktop peek zone when peekable
transitions.effect: state becomes peeking until pointer/focus leaves
width is clamped to minWidth/maxWidth; crossing inward below minWidth collapses, dragging outward to minWidth expands
Home collapses; End expands at maxWidth
toggles controlled callback and internal open state
temporarily opens; blur closes unless explicitly opened or descendant has data-active
track translates to active child index; inactive children become hidden/inert

unknowns.reason: SSR always evaluates isMobile=false, while the first client render reads matchMedia synchronously. A narrow viewport can therefore choose desktop aside on the server and mobile portal/nav on hydration; no identical-hydration or browser-pass claim is made.

Mobile, CollapsibleContent, and SlidingView apply/remove inert imperatively in ref callbacks. aria-hidden is SSR-observable, but inert presence before/through hydration must be browser-observed and is not asserted action-free.
Exact portal placement, focus restoration ordering, and focusout interactions require a browser; behavior is documented from canonical runtime but no browser pass is claimed.
CollapsibleTrigger requires a render ReactElement. The generic JSON composition DSL cannot encode a ReactElement-valued prop, so trigger linkage is not fabricated in the action-free vector.
The public direction prop is declared and defaults left, but canonical runtime computes translation solely from active child index; no direction-dependent observable effect is invented.

missingOperations.reason: draft browser-service semantics require canonical browser proof
